title: Dance Your Heart Out Day!
story: Every April 29, dancers around the world celebrate International Dance Day by moving to the beat! Professional dance companies put on special performances, while dance studios host open classes where anyone can try ballet, hip-hop, or salsa. Schools organize dance-offs in gymnasiums, and social media fills with videos of people showing off their best moves. Some cities even close streets for massive dance parties where hundreds of people groove together. Whether you're a trained dancer or someone who just loves to wiggle, this day encourages everyone to express themselves through movement and discover the joy that comes from dancing.
activity_question: What do dance studios typically do on International Dance Day?
activity_choices: Host open classes for anyone to try
activity_answer: Host open classes for anyone to try
follow_up_question: What dance style would you most like to learn and why?

title: Shrimp Scampi Gets Its Special Day!
story: Garlic sizzles in butter while shrimp dance around hot pans across America on April 29! National Shrimp Scampi Day celebrates this delicious Italian-American dish that makes kitchens smell amazing. Restaurants offer special scampi deals, and home cooks share their secret recipes online. The dish combines juicy shrimp with garlic, butter, white wine, and pasta for a meal that tastes fancy but is surprisingly easy to make.
activity_question: What makes kitchens smell amazing when people cook shrimp scampi?
bonus_fact: Scampi actually means "little lobster" in Italian!

title: Zip It Up for Zipper Day!
story: Every zipper on your jacket, backpack, and jeans gets its moment to shine on April 29 during National Zipper Day! People celebrate this handy invention by sharing photos of their coolest zippers, from sparkly ones on boots to giant zippers on sleeping bags. Some folks even wear as many zippered items as possible or post zipper art online. It's amazing how this simple metal track keeps our stuff secure and our clothes closed every single day!
activity_question: How do people celebrate National Zipper Day?
activity_choices: By sharing photos of their coolest zippers
By never using zippers all day
activity_answer: By sharing photos of their coolest zippers
bonus_fact: The zipper was originally called a "clasp locker" when it was first invented!

title: Making Dreams Come True Day
story: Every April 29, people around the world celebrate World Wish Day by supporting kids with serious illnesses and their biggest dreams. This special day started when the Make-A-Wish Foundation was created to grant wishes for children facing tough medical challenges. Today, thousands of volunteers, donors, and supporters share stories on social media, raise money, and help make magical moments happen. Some kids wish to meet their favorite celebrities, visit amazing places, or even become superheroes for a day. Communities organize fundraising events, and people wear blue to show their support for wish kids everywhere.
activity_question: What do people often wear on World Wish Day to show their support?
bonus_fact: The first wish ever granted was for a boy who wanted to be a police officer.
